command.faultline.resetSettings: FaultLine: Reset All Settings to Default
command.faultline.factoryReset: FaultLine: Factory Reset (Clear All Data & Settings)
walkthrough.faultline.title: Get Started with FaultLine
walkthrough.faultline.description: Configure FaultLine to play sounds on failures
walkthrough.faultline.step1.title: Test the Sound
walkthrough.faultline.step1.description: Run the 'Play Test Sound' command to verify audio is working.
walkthrough.faultline.step2.title: Choose Your Sound
walkthrough.faultline.step2.description: Pick from built-in sound packs or select your own custom sound.
walkthrough.faultline.step3.title: Configure Settings
walkthrough.faultline.step3.description: Adjust volume, quiet hours, and which events trigger sounds.
config.faultline.title: FaultLine
config.faultline.enabled: Turn FaultLine on or off. If off, no sounds will play.
config.faultline.soundPath: Path to your custom sound file. Leave empty to use the default faultline.mp3!
config.faultline.soundFolder: Path to a folder with many sounds. FaultLine will pick one at random.
config.faultline.sounds.task: Sound for when a task fails (like a build).
config.faultline.sounds.shell: Sound for when a terminal command fails.
config.faultline.sounds.terminal: Sound for when a terminal window closes with an error.
config.faultline.sounds.diagnostics: Sound for when new errors appear in your code.
config.faultline.sounds.longTask: Sound for tasks that take a long time to finish.
config.faultline.successEnabled: Play a happy sound when a build or test succeeds.
config.faultline.successSound: The sound to play on success.
config.faultline.volumes.task: Volume for tasks (-1 uses the main volume).
config.faultline.volumes.shell: Volume for terminal commands (-1 uses the main volume).
config.faultline.volumes.terminal: Volume for terminal windows (-1 uses the main volume).
config.faultline.volumes.diagnostics: Volume for diagnostic errors (-1 uses the main volume).
config.faultline.volumes.build: Volume for build errors (-1 uses the main volume).
config.faultline.volumes.longTask: Volume for long tasks (-1 uses the main volume).
config.faultline.volumeCurve: How the volume feels (Logarithmic is usually better).
config.faultline.showNotification: Show a popup message when an error happens.
config.faultline.notificationLevel: How important the popup should look.
config.faultline.sources: Where should FaultLine listen for errors?
config.faultline.sources.diagnostics: Listen to errors in your code editor.
config.faultline.sources.longTask: Listen to tasks that take a long time to finish.
config.faultline.cooldownMs: Minimum wait time between two sounds (in milliseconds).
config.faultline.cooldownPerSource: Wait separately for each type of error.
config.faultline.maxPerMinute: Maximum sounds allowed per minute (0 means no limit).
config.faultline.ignorePatterns: Words or patterns to ignore (no sound will play for these).
config.faultline.showStatusBar: Show the FaultLine icon in the bottom bar.
config.faultline.statusBarCounter: Show today's error count in the bottom bar.
config.faultline.flashStatusBar: Flash the bottom bar red when a sound plays.
config.faultline.quietHours.enabled: Enable quiet hours (no sounds during specific times).
config.faultline.muteWhenFocused: Don't play sound if you are already looking at VS Code.
config.faultline.snoozeMinutes: How long to snooze for when using the Snooze command.
config.faultline.diagnosticsThreshold: How many new errors are needed to play a sound.
config.faultline.longTaskThresholdMs: How long a task must run before it's considered 'long'.
config.faultline.logLevel: How much detail to show in the logs.
config.faultline.historyMax: How many recent errors to remember in history.
config.faultline.speakLabel: Have your computer speak the name of the error.
config.faultline.webhookUrl: Send error info to another app (via Webhook).
config.faultline.webhookAllowedDomains: Optional allowlist of host names for the Webhook URL. Empty array means any domain is allowed.
config.faultline.aiSummary.enabled: Use AI to explain why your code failed.
config.faultline.aiProvider: Which AI service to ask. 'Copilot' uses your existing Copilot subscription; the rest need their own free or paid API key.
config.faultline.openrouterApiKey: OpenRouter API key (legacy plain-text storage). Use 'FaultLine: Configure AI Provider' instead.
config.faultline.openrouterModel: Model id used when OpenRouter is the active AI provider.
config.faultline.groqModel: Model id used when Groq is the active AI provider.
config.faultline.geminiModel: Model id used when Google Gemini is the active AI provider.
config.faultline.huggingfaceModel: Model id used when Hugging Face is the active AI provider.
config.faultline.mistralModel: Model id used when Mistral AI is the active AI provider.
config.faultline.togetherModel: Model id used when Together AI is the active AI provider.
config.faultline.cohereModel: Model id used when Cohere is the active AI provider.
config.faultline.openaiModel: Model id used when OpenAI is the active AI provider.
config.faultline.anthropicModel: Model id used when Anthropic Claude is the active AI provider.
config.faultline.dailySummary: Show a summary of today's errors at 6:00 PM.
config.faultline.streakCounter: Cheer you on for consecutive successes!
config.faultline.bossFightMode: Turn coding into a boss fight (lose HP on errors)!
